description: Weather forecasts from the High-Resolution Rapid Refresh (HRRR) model operated by NOAA NWS NCEP.
attribution: NOAA NWS NCEP HRRR data processed by dynamical.org from NOAA Open Data Dissemination archives.
description_summary:
This dataset is an archive of past and present HRRR forecasts, optimized for spatial (map) access patterns. Forecasts are identified by an initialization time (`init_time`) denoting the start time of the model run, and step forward hourly along the `lead_time` dimension out to 48 hours. This dataset contains only the 00, 06, 12, and 18 hour UTC initialization times which produce the full length, 48 hour forecast.

This dataset uses the native HRRR Lambert Conformal Conic projection, with spatial indexing along the `x` and `y` dimensions. The example notebook shows how to use the embedded spatial reference to select geographic areas of interest.

Note: `dynamical-catalog>=0.7.0` (or `zarr>=3.2 icechunk>=2.0 gribberish>=1.5`) is required.
description_details:
### Source

The source grib files this archive is constructed from are provided by [NOAA Open Data Dissemination (NODD)](https://www.noaa.gov/information-technology/open-data-dissemination) and accessed from the [AWS Open Data Registry](https://registry.opendata.aws/noaa-hrrr-pds/). Operational data is additionally accessed from [NOAA NOMADS](https://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/).

### Data availability

The archive spans init times from 2018-07-13T12 UTC to the present. A number of
fields change behavior at the HRRR version 3 → version 4 upgrade on 2020-12-02,
and some variables are incomplete before that date.

See the [validation report](https://dynamical.org/catalog/noaa-hrrr-forecast-48-hour-virtual/validation/)
for complete details.

### Storage

Storage for this dataset is generously provided by [AWS Open Data](https://aws.amazon.com/opendata/).

### Chunks

This dataset is stored in [Zarr](https://zarr.dev/) format, which splits each variable into a grid of **chunks** — the smallest unit read from storage. When possible, aligning your reads with this dataset's chunk grid can significantly improve data access speed.

The element count and coordinate span of this dataset:

| dimension | chunk |
|---|---|
| init_time | 1 (6 hours) |
| lead_time | 1 (1 hour) |
| y | 1059 (3177 km) |
| x | 1799 (5397 km) |
| **uncompressed** | 14.5 MiB |
description_model: The High-Resolution Rapid Refresh (HRRR) is a NOAA real-time 3-km resolution, hourly updated, cloud-resolving, convection-allowing atmospheric model, initialized by 3km grids with 3km radar assimilation. Radar data is assimilated in the HRRR every 15 min over a 1-h period adding further detail to that provided by the hourly data assimilation from the 13km radar-enhanced Rapid Refresh.

variants.label: dynamical-catalog
variants.code:
import dynamical_catalog  # dynamical-catalog>=0.7.0

ds = dynamical_catalog.open("noaa-hrrr-forecast-48-hour-virtual", chunks=None)
ds["temperature_2m"].sel(init_time="2025-01-01T00", lead_time="24h")

# Variables with a vertical dimension live in the pressure_level and model_level groups
ds_pressure = dynamical_catalog.open("noaa-hrrr-forecast-48-hour-virtual", group="pressure_level", chunks=None)
ds_model = dynamical_catalog.open("noaa-hrrr-forecast-48-hour-virtual", group="model_level", chunks=None)

ds_pressure["temperature"].sel(pressure_level=500)

variants.label: pystac + icechunk
variants.code:
import icechunk
import pystac
import xarray as xr

catalog = pystac.Catalog.from_file("https://stac.dynamical.org/catalog.json")
collection = catalog.get_child("noaa-hrrr-forecast-48-hour-virtual")
asset = collection.assets["icechunk-https"]

authorize = icechunk.containers_credentials({"s3://noaa-hrrr-bdp-pds/": icechunk.s3_anonymous_credentials()})
repo = icechunk.Repository.open(
    icechunk.http_storage(asset.href),
    authorize_virtual_chunk_access=authorize,
)
session = repo.readonly_session("main")

ds = xr.open_zarr(session.store, chunks=None)
ds["temperature_2m"].sel(init_time="2025-01-01T00", lead_time="24h")

# Variables with a vertical dimension live in the pressure_level and model_level groups
ds_pressure = xr.open_zarr(session.store, group="pressure_level", chunks=None)
ds_model = xr.open_zarr(session.store, group="model_level", chunks=None)

ds_pressure["temperature"].sel(pressure_level=500)

echo_top.comment: -999 encodes no echo; CF-aware readers mask it to NaN.

lightning_threat_1m.comment: GSD maximum lightning threat 1 derived from upward graupel flux, in flashes km-2 (5 min)-1. Encoded in HRRR GRIB as LTNGSD (lightning strike density) at the pseudo-level 1 m above ground. Before the 2022-06-28T12Z cycle this GRIB slot carried lightning potential index (J kg-1), a different quantity that is not included.

lightning_threat_2m.comment: GSD maximum lightning threat 2 derived from vertically integrated ice, in flashes km-2 (5 min)-1. Encoded in HRRR GRIB as LTNGSD (lightning strike density) at the pseudo-level 2 m above ground. Before the 2022-06-28T12Z cycle this GRIB slot carried lightning potential index (J kg-1), a different quantity that is not included.

mass_density_8m.comment: Near-surface smoke concentration. Source values at init_times before 2021-12-21T18Z are in ug m-3; NOAA corrected the encoding to kg m-3 from that cycle onward, so multiply earlier values by 1e-9 to compare.

maximum_wind_u_component_10m.long_name: Maximum 10 metre wind speed u component

maximum_wind_v_component_10m.long_name: Maximum 10 metre wind speed v component

percent_frozen_precipitation_surface.comment: -50 encodes no/undefined frozen precipitation; CF-aware readers mask it to NaN.

pressure_of_lifted_parcel_level_255_0mb.long_name: Pressure of level from which parcel was lifted

relative_humidity_with_respect_to_precip.long_name: Relative humidity with respect to precipitable water

vegetation_type_surface.comment: MODIS-IGBP 20-category land-use classification (WRF MODIFIED_IGBP_MODIS_NOAH), as used by the HRRR land surface model.

model_level/mass_density.comment: Smoke concentration. Source values at init_times before 2021-12-21T18Z are in ug m-3; NOAA corrected the encoding to kg m-3 from that cycle onward, so multiply earlier values by 1e-9 to compare.

model_level/number_concentration_ice_fri.comment: Climatological ice-friendly (non-hygroscopic, dust-like) aerosol number concentration from the Thompson-Eidhammer aerosol-aware microphysics, encoded in HRRR GRIB as PMTC 'Particulate matter (coarse)'.

model_level/number_concentration_water_f.comment: Climatological water-friendly (hygroscopic) aerosol number concentration from the Thompson-Eidhammer aerosol-aware microphysics, encoded in HRRR GRIB as PMTF 'Particulate matter (fine)'.

title: NOAA HRRR forecast, 48 hour, virtual
